results.title: Conscience in conflict
results.creator_credit: Columbia Pictures and Learning Corporation of America
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Great Themes of Literature”.
At the opening of this edited version of the film ‘Man for all Seasons’, based on Robert Bolt’s play, Sir Thomas More is about to be executed. He has chosen to die rather than compromise his religious beliefs, the principles which he feels constitute his very identity. He is making an existential stand. The commentator for the series, Orson Welles, describes Sir Thomas More’s dilemma. King Henry VIII has decided to divorce Catherine of Aragon and marry Anne Boleyn. The King wants England’s nobility to support his decision by endorsing the Act of Supremacy. Next to the King, Sir Thomas More is the most powerful man in England and his approval is of the utmost importance. However, herein lies the conflict - More cannot condone either the King’s divorce or his usurpation of the Church’s authority. He chooses silence as a middle course. Suitable for middle and upper secondary levels.

results.title: Authority and rebellion
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Great Themes of Literature”.
This specially edited version of the film adapted from Herman Wouk’s The Caine Mutiny explores the first half of the novel - the events leading up to the mutiny. As in the novel, the film focuses on a young naval officer who has been taught to revere authority, but faced with an authority that has abnegated its responsibilities, goes along with the rebellion.

results.title: The Prophecies of Nostradamus
results.creator_credit: Antonia Benedek and British Broadcasting Corporation
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Great Mysteries (Ep 3)”.
In this film, Henry Lincoln breaks through the barriers set up by innumerable interpreters which has obscured the real Nostradamus and reveals what the man himself was like and what he actually wrote.

results.title: W.A. Mozart
results.creator_credit: Jeremy Marre, John Seabourne and Seabourne Enterprises
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Great composers”
The life and work of Mozart is discussed and excerpts from his music are interspersed throughout the film. This film features the London Mozart Players and Russian pianist, Nina Milkina. Introduction by Nina Milkina.

results.title: Mozart in Salzburg
results.creator_credit: Barrie Gavin, Francis Hitching, Francis Hitching Productions and Profilm
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Great Composers”.
Biographical film on the life of one of the world’s greatest composers, Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart, born in 1756 in Salzburg, Austria. The brilliance and genius of Mozart is still appreciated and interpreted today by musicians worldwide. Features the Salzburg Mozarteum Orchestra in this specially staged concert program, conducted by Ralph Weikert and distinguished broadcaster and musicologist Antony Hopkins who provides an introduction to each concert piece, including a brief account of Mozart’s life from childhood to adulthood.

results.title: Beethoven
results.creator_credit: Jeremy Marre and Anvil Films and Record Group
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Great Composers”.
This film deals with Beethoven’s music up to 1806. John Ogden at the piano plays the “Appassionata” from the “Tempest Sonata” and with Brenda Lucas the early “Sonata for four hands”. Von Karajan conducts Symphony no. 3 (the “Eroica”) op. 55 and the Gabrielli Quartet plays “Rasoumoffsky Quartet” no.2 op.59.

group_works.title: True Australians

results.title: The Find of '93
results.creator_credit: R.H. Miller and Southern Cross Newsreels
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Golden West Series”.
Paddy Hannan’s find in 1893 led to the development of Kalgoorlie. Over 28 million ounces of fine gold have been taken from the “Golden Mile’s” open cut and deep mines. Demonstrates the mining process from power drilling of the ore bearing rock to crushing, screening and grinding of the ore. Washing and treating before processing in flotation cells, storage before smelting and finally the bullion is produced ready for the last smelting at the Royal Mint.
